bullets: Conceptual governance study of organizations that delegate decision authority to large language models and agentic AI in high-consequence settings, evaluated on synthetic stress tests rather than field data.

No specific model is used or named; the paper formulates AI delegation as a governance-aware partially observable Markov decision process with Bayesian state estimation, benchmarked against five alternative governance strategies.
Sequential Bayesian governance is the strongest general-purpose policy across heterogeneous AI-quality regimes, while specialized heuristics perform well only in stationary settings.

Survey of the financial large language model literature, organizing work into six layers from financial corpora and domain modeling to retrieval grounding, multimodal integration, and agentic workflow automation.
As a survey it uses no model of its own; it reviews domain-adapted, instruction-tuned, retrieval-augmented, and agentic financial systems along with their benchmarks and risk-control practices.

Argues the next generation of financial models should be evaluated as auditable decision-support systems operating under temporal, regulatory, and economic constraints rather than as isolated text generators.

Conceptual marketing framework for how AI assistants surface personal brands, with a cross-system comparison of ChatGPT, Claude, and Perplexity, drawing on reported industry data from December 2025 to June 2026.

No empirical model test is run; the paper proposes a six-signal model of citation eligibility covering reputation, source colocation, structured presence, distinctiveness, freshness, and crawl accessibility, weighted by citation likelihood.
Concludes that the three systems weight sources differently so visibility must be pursued engine by engine, and that relevant indicators shift from traffic to revenue and branded search.
Three public customer-service dialogue datasets used to extract process-mining event logs from unstructured enterprise conversations, with individual conversations as the unit of extraction.

A retrieval-extraction-verification pipeline with RAG grounding, ten-pass self-consistency voting, and a separate verification model extracts activities; the underlying model family is not named. Evaluated by F1 against dataset labels.
Reaches average F1 of 93 percent, surpassing an NLI-BART baseline by 4 percentage points and standalone LLM methods by 8 percentage points.
validation_note: F1 on three dialogue datasets vs NLI-BART
